message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~87 reviews on the restaurant listing (parent Surf Club venue ~321); OpenTable listing live; Tripadvisor listing (under former name 'Navigator') shows only 1 review — overall low to moderate review volume for a high-profile opening
- Sentiment: mostly positive, with mixed service-execution notes
- Longevity: Restaurant concept opened with the Surf Club venue in 2024; rebranded to 'Navigator' November 2024 under Michael Hung; reverted to 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club' after Hung's departure (per search snippet dated Jan 2026) [UNVERIFIED on exact departure date and reversion timing]. Current artisanal menu credited to 'Chef Mondragon' [UNVERIFIED full name].
- Credentials: ["Cited by Locale Magazine as one of the most consequential new Palm Springs openings","Featured in Bon Traveler's Best Restaurants in Palm Springs","Chef Michael Hung's prior properties (Colony Club, SO.PA at L'Horizon) earned Michelin Guide listings — credential attaches to Hung, not directly to this restaurant"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant', 'best new Palm Springs restaurants 2025', and 'surf-inspired Palm Springs'. Does NOT consistently appear in 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Desert' (wrong city/corridor), or generic 'date night Palm Springs' top-of-list — those are still dominated by Copley's, Workshop, The Purple Room, Mr. Lyons, Le Vallauris.
- Site structured data: Own site (palmspringssurfclub.com/the-restaurant/) carries clean address, phone, hours, Resy/OpenTable reservation hooks, and segmented Lunch/Dinner/Brunch menus, but no visible schema.org Restaurant JSON-LD detected in fetched content. Menus are linked as PDFs (e.g., Rest-HH-Menu.pdf) — agent-readable but not structured. Tripadvisor listing is unclaimed.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
# The Palm Springs Surf Club - Restaurant Information

**Restaurant Name:**
"The Restaurant" at The Palm Springs Surf Club

**Chef Name:**
Not mentioned in the provided content.

**Menu Structure:**
The establishment offers three distinct menus:
- Brunch Menu
- Lunch Menu
- Dinner Menu

All are available as downloadable PDFs on their website.

**Reservation Links:**
- Primary reservations: Resy (resy.com/cities/palm-springs-ca/venues/the-palm-springs-surf-club)
- Large parties (20+): Direct phone call required
- Takeout ordering: Toast Tab online platform

**Address:**
1500 S Gene Autry Trail, Palm Springs, CA 92264

**Phone:**
(760) 205-3634

**Hours of Operation:**
- Thursday-Friday: 11am-8pm
- Saturday-Sunday: 10am-8pm
- Drifters (casual dining): Hours vary; call for current times

**Cuisine Style:**
Described as "Fresh, Flavorful, California Inspired" featuring "Surf & Turf – California Seasonal Cuisine" emphasizing locally sourced, seasonal ingredients.

**Notable Details:**
- Open to the public with no admission fee
- Partial views of the surf pool; terrace access for better views
- Pets allowed on patio/terrace only
- Happy hour available in bar area

No schema.org JSON-LD or microdata structures were evident in the provided HTML content.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding Navigator at the Palm Springs Surf Club and Chef Michael Hung:

## Navigator Restaurant Overview

Navigator, the flagship restaurant of the Palm Springs Surf Club, officially opened on Friday, November 15, 2024, offering a sophisticated dining experience led by acclaimed Executive Chef Michael Hung. Navigator features modern American cuisine infused with global surf-inspired flavors from destinations like Morocco, Mexico, and Hawaii.

## Chef Michael Hung's Background

Chef Hung's resume includes prestigious roles at New York's Daniel and Aquavit, as well as in San Francisco at Jardinière (under Chef Traci Des Jardins) and La Folie (with Chef Roland Passot), both of which received Michelin recognition.

## Departure in 2025

Regarding the departure mentioned in your query, Chef Michael Hung has left the Palm Springs Surf Club, according to a review from January 2026. However, the search results do not provide specific details about the timing or reasons for his departure in 2025.

The Palm Springs Surf Club website now refers to their main dining venue as "The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club" rather than Navigator, suggesting there may have been changes to the restaurant's name or leadership following Chef Hung's departure.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant:

**Restaurant Overview:**
The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club features a curated artisanal menu by Chef Mondragon, with indoor and outdoor seating offering breathtaking views of the surf and mountains.

**Dining Philosophy:**
The chefs prepare fresh, seasonal ingredients where surf meets turf, with a menu that reflects the essence of the Golden State and celebrates sun-drenched fields, coastal waters and valley gardens, embracing a philosophy of fresh, vibrant and locally sourced product.

**2026 Menu Items:**
Crowd favorites include Surf Club Ceviche, Pork Schnitzel, and Lemon Meringue Pavlova. The Surf Club Ceviche features snapper and sea bass with lime and crispy taro root, the Pork Schnitzel comes with Romesco sauce, and the Lemon Meringue Pavlova is a light, airy dessert balanced with berry coulis.

**Location & Atmosphere:**
The restaurant is located at 1500 S Gene Autry Trail and features floor-to-ceiling glass walls with views of the lazy river and wave pool.

**Additional Dining:**
The venue also offers Drifters, a poolside oasis with outdoor dining and a creative beverage program featuring breakfast, lunch, and dinner options.

**Workshop Kitchen & Bar**: Located at 800 N. Palm Canyon Drive, Palm Springs, CA 92262, Workshop Kitchen & Bar is a restaurant featuring upscale dining. The outdoor patio at Workshop is perfect during the warmer months for a late-night dinner, with new American eats and seasonal cocktails.

**Copley's**: Copley's on Palm Canyon is among the restaurants in Palm Springs with a great view. The search results show positive visitor reviews mentioning the restaurant.

**Mr. Lyons**: Mr. Lyons has been around in some form or another since the 1940s, and after a massive renovation in 2015, this historic steakhouse has retaken its place as the desert's go-to spot for breaking out your best pearls, sipping martinis like they're tap water, and going to town on giant slabs of meat.

**Le Vallauris**: If your bucket list includes dining at the most expensive restaurants in the U.S. the luscious stylings of Le Vallauris Restaurant will show you how premium Palm Springs restaurants add prestige to the local dining culture, located in Downtown Palm Springs.

All of these establishments appear to be recognized among Palm Springs' best dining options for 2026.

The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club is now open! It's a one-of-a-kind dining experience where California's fresh, seasonal flavors meet the laid-back spirit of surf culture.

The menu draws inspiration from both land and sea, offering surf & turf dishes made with fresh, locally sourced ingredients, including bright ceviches, seared seafood, expertly grilled steaks and plant-based options.

I've found search results for the Palm Springs Surf Club restaurants. Here's what I discovered:

## Restaurant Overview

The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club features Californian coastal-inspired cuisine, nestled within the luxurious Palm Springs Surf Club with stunning views of the San Jacinto Mountains and a menu focused on fresh, locally sourced ingredients.

## Reviews and Ratings

**Yelp:** The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club has 119 photos and 87 reviews on Yelp, located at 1500 S Gene Autry Trl, Palm Springs, CA 92264.

**TripAdvisor:** The restaurant features Southern Californian coastal-inspired cuisine, offering stunning views of the San Jacinto Mountains with a menu focused on fresh, locally sourced ingredients.

## Navigator Restaurant

The search results also mention "Navigator At The Palm Springs Surf Club" on TripAdvisor, which appears to be another name for the dining establishment at the venue.

## Dining Experience

The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club offers a sophisticated yet laid-back vibe with unique and tasty food and drinks. Reviewers highlighted favorites like the flatbread, curry, croquettes, sea bass, Surf Club Ceviche, Spring Vegetable Risotto, Pork Schnitzel with Romesco sauce, and Lemon Meringue Pavlova.

## Hours and Reservations

Reservations are strongly suggested but not required, and can be booked through OpenTable. There is no fee to enter the Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club.

content.text: Crucial finding: Locale Magazine featured "Amala Restaurant & Bar" — yet another former/parallel name for the venue — as one of the top 12 openings, NOT specifically "The Restaurant" or "Navigator." This restaurant has gone through THREE name changes (Amala → Navigator → The Restaurant), which the assessment didn't capture.

confirms: Yelp shows ~87 reviews on The Restaurant listing and ~321 reviews on parent Palm Springs Surf Club listing — confirmed verbatim in Yelp page titles
Tripadvisor listing under 'Navigator At The Palm Springs Surf Club' shows only 1 review and is unclaimed by owner — confirmed
OpenTable listing is live for 'The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club' — confirmed
Restaurant rebranded to Navigator under Chef Michael Hung in November 2024 (Nov 15, 2024 grand opening) — confirmed via multiple sources (PS Tribune, Daily Ovation, LA FlavRReport)
Chef Hung's prior Michelin-recognized credentials (Daniel, Aquavit, Jardinière, La Folie) attach to Hung personally, not directly to this restaurant — confirmed
Surfaces reliably for 'Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant' queries
Does NOT appear in Bon Traveler's 20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs list — independent fetch confirms exclusion (this actually contradicts the credential claim — see contradicts)
Current menu credited to 'Chef Mondragon' — confirmed via restaurant's own description; full name still not surfaced
Menus served as PDFs (Lunch-Menu-2.pdf, Dinner-Menu.pdf in /wp-content/uploads/2025/10/) — confirmed in JSON-LD FAQPage
contradicts: Claim: 'Featured in Bon Traveler's Best Restaurants in Palm Springs' — REFUTED. Direct fetch of Bon Traveler's 20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs article shows no mention of The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club, Navigator, or Amala. The venue is absent from that list.

Claim: 'Cited by Locale Magazine as one of the most consequential new Palm Springs openings' — PARTIALLY REFUTED / MISATTRIBUTED. Locale Magazine's feature was for 'Amala Restaurant & Bar' (an earlier/different brand name for the Surf Club's dining venue per the property's own Facebook post), not for 'The Restaurant' or 'Navigator.' The credential attaches to a prior brand identity, which the assessment did not disclose. Additionally, this reveals the venue has had THREE name iterations: Amala → Navigator → The Restaurant, not two as the assessment states.

Claim: 'no visible schema.org Restaurant JSON-LD detected in fetched content' — PARTIALLY REFUTED. The page DOES carry substantial JSON-LD via Yoast schema graph: WebPage, WebSite, Organization, BreadcrumbList, ImageObject, NewsArticle, and a rich FAQPage with 14 Q&A entries covering hours, cuisine type, reservations, pets, kids, menus. What's missing is specifically a schema.org/Restaurant type with menu/address/openingHours properties — the correct phrasing is 'no Restaurant-type JSON-LD; address/hours data is only in FAQ prose and rendered HTML.'
flagged_as_unverified: Exact date of Michael Hung's departure and the exact reversion timing to 'The Restaurant' name — no source confirms specific dates; January 2026 review snippet is the only data point
Chef Mondragon's full name — no source surfaces it

Sentiment characterization 'mostly positive with mixed service-execution notes' — not independently sampled at scale; the one Tripadvisor review (4.0, March 2026) does match the 'mixed service' pattern but n=1
Whether the venue surfaces in 'best new Palm Springs restaurants 2025' query results — not directly tested in this verification pass
Visibility gap score of 7 and 'anchor-reference' convertibility tier — methodology-dependent, cannot be independently verified
input.notes: Two credentials are misattributed or wrong: Bon Traveler does NOT list the venue (verified by direct fetch), and Locale Magazine's feature was for 'Amala' — a third, undisclosed prior brand for the same venue. The assessment missed an entire prior name (Amala) in the rebrand history, making the longevity narrative incomplete: Amala → Navigator (Nov 2024, Hung) → The Restaurant (post-Hung). The structured-data claim needs sharpening: the site has substantial JSON-LD (FAQPage, Organization, WebPage, NewsArticle) but no Restaurant-typed schema with menu/address/openingHours — the agent-readability picture is better than 'no schema' implies. Yelp review counts (87 / 321) and unclaimed Tripadvisor listing (1 review) verified verbatim.
